Video.summary: Time-saving quick tips to feed your crafting craving.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Learn to sew, step-by-step with National Sewing Circle experts on CraftsyTV.
Video.grandparentTitle: National Sewing Circle presented by CraftsyTV
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Learn to knit, step-by-step with The Knitting Circle experts on CraftsyTV.
Video.grandparentTitle: The Knitting Circle Presented by CraftsyTV
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Today we take a very personal approach using our names and monograms. Our first projects are monogram ornaments made from air dry clay. Then make initial letter necklaces for you and your friends. Today's art journal technique is lettering -- learn some tips for simple calligraphy. Finally make an initial bulletin board covered with initials and doodle art.
Video.title: Lettering

Video.summary: -Cordless drills are a staple in many woodworking shops and deserve a dedicated storage stand. George Vondriska shows you how to make your own cordless drill stand, fully equipped with a power strip for easy charging, plus storage drawers for tips and bits. This project is great for practicing biscuit joinery and hand tool work.
Video.grandparentTitle: Woodworkers Guild of America presented by CraftsyTV
Video.title: Cordless Drill Stand

Video.summary: Creative Crochet Corner is an online video streaming service all about learning how to crochet! Whether you're a new to crochet just learning the basics or an experienced crocheter looking for a challenge, Creative Crochet Corner is here to help. We offer a vast library of video tutorials taught by talented crochet experts who guide you through each technique step-by-step, sharing their best tips and tricks.
Video.grandparentTitle: Creative Crochet Corner presented by CraftsyTV
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Author Laura Poplin combines the ancient art of leatherwork and chainmaille to make beautiful contemporary jewelry. Learn to make colorful dyed and painted leather rectangles and finish them into jewelry using classic leatherworking techniques. Laura shows how to connect leather pieces with Byzantine chainmaile weave to make a cuff style bracelet. Katie demonstrates a quick and easy cup chain bracelet.
Video.title: Leather and Chainmaille Jewelry

Video.summary: George Vondriska shows you how to create a handmade ring stand that's perfect for storing rings next to the sink, on a bedside table, or even in the workshop. Then, George demonstrates how to construct a custom photo frame to hold your favorite memories. Finally, learn to make an attractive cutting board that makes an excellent handmade gift.
Video.grandparentTitle: Woodworkers Guild of America presented by CraftsyTV
Video.title: Ring Stand, Picture Frame, & Cutting Board

Image.alt: Ring Stand, Picture Frame, & Cutting Board
Ring Stand, Picture Frame, & Cutting Board
Ring Stand, Picture Frame, & Cutting Board

Video.summary: Not all artists use brushes, and paint is not their only medium of choice. On today's episode, we begin with a lesson inspired by Camille Pissarro as we paint using a palette knife instead of a brush. Then meet Dina Wakley for a graffiti inspired, modern street art design. We finish up with Cheryl Boglioli and a 3D sculpture using an interesting medium to create a female form reminiscent of modernism.
Video.title: Mediums

Video.summary: We're having trouble seeing straight. Then again, it's probably the Drunkard's Path block! Angela's here to make sure the steps go "straight to your head."
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: The first episode of Fresh Quilting's new season features blocks - the most basic of quilt techniques. Ebony Love show how to use negative space in nontraditional blocks. Then, Lee Chappell Monroe demonstrates basic techniques for attaching blocks together. Eliane Bergmann will close each episode with a sewing machine tip to elevate your quilting. Her tip for this episode is quilt as you go and how to create panels with scraps and turn them into coasters, pouches and more.
Video.title: Blocks

Video.summary: Learn to quilt step by step with National Quilting Circle experts on CraftsyTV.
Video.title: National Quilting Circle presented by CraftsyTV

Image.alt: National Quilting Circle presented by CraftsyTV
National Quilting Circle presented by CraftsyTV
National Quilting Circle presented by CraftsyTV

Video.summary: This week's woven lattice quilt might look intricate, but it's actually perfect for beginners!
Video.title: Weave it Be

Video.summary: The Walters household is eating healthy this week, but that doesn't mean Angela's quilts have to be. Today she is whipping up the Milky Way Quilt by Monique Dillard featuring Lily & Loom fabric.
Video.title: Milky Way

Video.summary: This is not a drill! Angela has heard your requests loud and clear, and she's finally making the Bargello quilt that everyone's been asking for! So break out the layer cakes and prepare to get crazy with precuts.
Video.title: Modern Bargello

Video.summary: Believe it or not, this quilt is only made of straight seams! Watch it "unfold" as Angela conquers the Strip City pattern with Boundless blues.
Video.title: Strip City

Video.summary: Are you a "slow and steady" quilter or more of a "forget perfect, finish fast" quilter like Angela? Tonight, she uses templates and block piecing with LOTS of triangles to create an out-of-this-world ombré quilt. Plus, she shares some handy tips for quilting with rulers.
Video.title: Colorful Geometric Quilt

Video.summary: Machine quilting isn't always as easy as it looks, but anyone can do these three free-motion quilting designs (promise)! Learn how wavy lines, starburst designs, and simple cursive words can turn your quilt into a work of art.
Video.title: Bonus: Top 3 Free-Motion Quilting Designs Even Beginners Can Do

Image.alt: Bonus: Top 3 Free-Motion Quilting Designs Even Beginners Can Do
Bonus: Top 3 Free-Motion Quilting Designs Even Beginners Can Do
Bonus: Top 3 Free-Motion Quilting Designs Even Beginners Can Do

Video.summary: Looking for a fun beginner-friendly quilt? Angela’s got you covered with a super simple pattern that leaves plenty of room for the quilting — easy cutting, easy piecing, easy peasy!
Video.title: Beginner-Friendly Negative Space Quilt

Video.summary: Angela's reminded of her age as she relies on her kids to keep up with the digital world. Watch as she uses two basic shapes to create a mod half-square triangle quilt that's easier to make than it looks.
Video.title: Modern Technology Half-Square Triangle Quilt

Video.summary: This week Angela is preparing to host a dinner for quilters, so of course, she has to quilt up a few placemats!
Video.title: Quilt-As-You-Go Placemats

Video.summary: Inspiration for today's fashion comes from many sources, including the glamour of the silver screen. Angela Wolf starts out with a luxurious chemise top; perfect under a jacket or sweater or glamorous all by itself for evening wear. Then it's time to bring out your inner princess as Cheryl Sleboda creates crowns - fit for a movie star, your favorite cosplay outfit, or a little girl's party.
Video.title: Movie Stars

Video.summary: Nations raced to put satellites into space and the fashion scene took a futuristic step toward figure-hugging fashions. Gretchen Hirsch shows how to redesign a pattern to make a trendy blouse that buttons up the back and designer Peggy Sagers takes a cue from a man's shirt to create a flattering shirtwaist dress with easy collar variations.
Video.title: The Space Race Begins - The Late 1950's

Image.alt: The Space Race Begins - The Late 1950's
The Space Race Begins - The Late 1950's
The Space Race Begins - The Late 1950's

Video.summary: Using parachute cord in a whole new way, author Ashley Bunting adds beads from her stash to make a charming, colorful floral brooch. Katie rides the floral jewelry wave with pendants made with leather flowers, wire and crystals.
Video.title: Trendspotting: Parachute Cord and Flowers

Video.summary: Author and instructor Melissa Grakowsky-Shippee creates stunning beaded components using two-hole tile beads and teaches her technique for a flawless herringbone stitch. Katie discusses the various types of memory wire as she makes a glass bead bracelet that's fun to wear.
Video.title: Spotlight on Bracelets

Video.summary: Save special treasures in pockets. Julie Fei-Fan Balzer brushes up page pockets with patterns. May Flaum mixes pockets into traditional pages. Joe Rotella has a stamping tip and Christine Drumheller goes way outside the pockets with embellishments.
Video.title: Episode 2: Pocket Scrapbooking

Video.summary: Scrapbooking can get very regimented – with the right way to use a product or the correct way to lay out a page. Today we're encouraging a more casual approach – one that fits your life style and design aesthetics. Take a casual approach to your scrapbooking with two "hello" themed pages, scrapbook sketches from Kelly Purkey, and five ways to use lattice designs from Sara Naumann.
Video.title: Casual

Video.summary: This season is all about inspiration, particularly from the art masters. We're not only inspired but we take that creativity one step further with new materials. We begin with a design that pays homage to Sigmar Polke, a contemporary artist that was known for using varied materials even glass. We make a stitched canvas. Then Joe Rotella is back, taking a page from Sigmar's book by using found objects like wood and license plates to make a modern birdhouse. We'll finish up with May Flaum and the post-impressionist era and a lesson on being inspired by art but styling it in your own way.
Video.title: Inspiration

Video.summary: Learn to bake and cook with CraftsyTV.
Video.title: Baking & Cooking

Video.summary: Hold onto your needles! Tracie and Jodi, are here to dish out fresh fiber inspiration. From project picks to wooly good gossip, things are about to get super knitty… and maybe just a little nutty.
Video.title: Episode 4

Video.summary: Forget wool and alpaca fiber; upcycled socks and grocery bags are where it's at! Tune in to find out how to turn any tubular (and cuttable) material into knittable yarn.
Video.title: UPCYCLED Knitting: Turn Denim, Cotton & Plastic into Yarn!

Image.alt: UPCYCLED Knitting: Turn Denim, Cotton & Plastic into Yarn!
UPCYCLED Knitting: Turn Denim, Cotton & Plastic into Yarn!
UPCYCLED Knitting: Turn Denim, Cotton & Plastic into Yarn!

Video.summary: Forget wool and alpaca fiber; upcycled socks and grocery bags are where it's at! Tune in to find out how to turn any tubular (and cuttable) material into knittable yarn.
Video.title: UPCYCLED Knitting: Turn Denim, Cotton & Plastic into Yarn!

Image.alt: UPCYCLED Knitting: Turn Denim, Cotton & Plastic into Yarn!
UPCYCLED Knitting: Turn Denim, Cotton & Plastic into Yarn!
UPCYCLED Knitting: Turn Denim, Cotton & Plastic into Yarn!

Video.summary: Beat single sock syndrome with a little multitasking! The girls go over how to knit two socks at once so you'll never end up with solo socks again.
Video.title: Multitasking for Knitters: Two-at-a-Time Socks

Video.summary: People keep life exciting. Jenny Barnett-Rohrs and a special friend show how to tag your best friends with matching lanyard tags. Candie Cooper celebrates diversity with whimsical cone kids. She also crafts one-of-a-kind wooden kokeshi dolls. Jenny uses age-old symbols to monoprint a family crest to frame or use for banners.
Video.title: People

Video.summary: Welcome to Artists's Academy where we are dedicated to making you a better artist. We believe everyone has an artist within, waiting to be unleashed. Our mission is to be your ultimate resource for visual art education using tutorials covering everything from fundamental techniques to advanced concepts in painting and drawing.
Video.title: Artist's Academy presented by CraftsyTV

Video.summary: Welcome to CraftsyTV's Cook & Create, where delectable delights meet crafty creators.
Video.title: Cook & Create
